insight: Qatar's performance in the 2026 World Cup has shown a notable trend when it comes to hydration breaks, with the team gaining momentum in both of the two breaks they've had, resulting in a mean increase of 0.150 points per minute. This suggests that the breaks have been beneficial for Qatar, allowing them to regroup and come back stronger. Notably, in the instances where Qatar was not pressing before the break, they were still able to gain momentum afterwards, with an average increase of 0.150 points per minute, indicating that the breaks have been a valuable opportunity for the team to reset and refocus. Overall, the data implies that hydration breaks have been a positive factor for Qatar, enabling them to build momentum and potentially change the course of the match in their favor.
